mentions.note: Steve Kolane (Kellogg breakup CEO) started Jan 1, 2026; split planned H2 2026; market ignoring potential (like Kellogg pre-split); Cramer doubts appetite for breakup but credits Kolane as right guy given track record; calls for action on CPG sector.

Caller Jim asks about Kraft (merged 2011); notes Warren Buffett divesting 28% stake; Cramer says new CEO Steve Carell came from Kellogg is okay, but he and Ben Stole can't get behind it—not enough good happening in food business; Cramer is a seller, not a buyer.
Food giant rallying on AI productivity story despite GLP-1 demand risk; solid earnings and buyback; benefiting from diversification rotation.
No growth despite CEO efforts; avoiding no-growth + yield combo; won't outperform despite good yield; looking for growth opportunities instead.
Down considerably; packaged food inflation indicator.
New CEO Cahillane executing Win/Hold/Win Big portfolio strategy; investing behind brands with competitive advantage; capacity utilization jumped 29%→54%.
